summary:
Iteration 12 — Payment-gated chat bug-fix (Iter 12) AND Iteration-11 P3 features (AI semantic search, ICS calendar, customer + admin CSV exports, Redis-less startup) fully verified end-to-end. Backend: 18/18 pytest cases PASS (test_iter12.py). Frontend: customer dashboard chat gating visually + behaviourally correct on /customer for the existing customer (16 bookings).

BACKEND — Payment-gated chat: GET /api/chat/{bid}/access returns {enabled:false, payment_status:'unpaid', reason:'...payment of the Platform Service Fee.'} on the unpaid booking 060a549a... for BOTH the customer and the artist; {enabled:true} on the paid booking cca6a262... for the customer; {enabled:true} on the unpaid booking for the admin (bypass for moderation). GET/POST /api/chat/{bid}/messages and POST /api/chat/{bid}/upload all return HTTP 403 with detail string starting EXACTLY 'Chat Access Denied — Complete Platform Service Fee payment to unlock chat.' when called by customer OR artist on the unpaid booking. Same endpoints succeed on the paid booking (GET /messages returns []). Admin call to /messages on the unpaid booking → 200. WEBSOCKET: wss://.../api/ws/chat/{unpaid_bid}?token=<customer_jwt> is rejected at handshake (server closes with code 4402 reason 'payment_required'); same URL on the paid booking opens and emits {event:'presence', joined:<user_id>, participants:[...]} as the first frame. Token decoding uses JWT_SECRET and rejects bad tokens (4001) / non-participants (4003) / missing booking (4004).

BACKEND — Iter 11 P3 features (first-ever test pass):
  • POST /api/search/ai {query:'Singer in Mumbai under 50000', limit:5} (anonymous, no auth) → 200 with JSON list of artists (≤5). Endpoint is robust to missing EMERGENT_LLM_KEY (graceful fallback to regex keyword filter; tested by simply hitting it — no 500).
  • GET /api/bookings/{paid_bid}/calendar.ics (customer JWT) → 200, Content-Type 'text/calendar; charset=utf-8', body starts with 'BEGIN:VCALENDAR' and contains BEGIN:VEVENT + END:VCALENDAR; UID built from booking id, SUMMARY 'BookTalent — <artist> · <event_type>', LOCATION = venue + city, attachment filename booking_<ref>.ics.
  • GET /api/exports/my-bookings.csv (customer JWT) → 200, Content-Type 'text/csv', first row header contains 'Ref' (full: 'Booking Ref, Date, Event Type, Status, Customer, Artist ID, Artist Fee, Platform Service Fee, GST, BookTalent Total, Amount Paid, Created At').
  • GET /api/admin/exports/revenue.csv (admin JWT, days=90 default) → 200, Content-Type 'text/csv', header includes 'Platform Fee (BT)'. Customer JWT → 403 (admin_only Depends() gated correctly).
  • Redis-less startup: GET /api/auth/login responds 200 — confirming ConnectionManager._ensure_redis() is lazy and does not crash when REDIS_URL is unset; in-process mode active. Multi-replica fan-out NOT tested per spec.

FRONTEND — Customer Dashboard /customer (logged in as customer@booktalent.com → 'Welcome, Rajesh', 16 bookings rendered): Paid booking (BT-260625-99978D, payment_status=token_paid) action button reads EXACTLY '💬 Chat' (enabled, title='Open chat'). Unpaid bookings (e.g. BT-260625-0E8B5D etc., payment_status=unpaid, status 'Pending payment') action button reads EXACTLY '🔒 Pay to Unlock Chat' (disabled=true, opacity 0.55, cursor not-allowed, title='Chat will be available after successful payment of the Platform Service Fee.'). Clicking the paid '💬 Chat' opens a modal ChatBox (data-testid='chat-box') — chat-locked card is NOT present (correct; payment_status is token_paid). Locked card defence path verified in code: ChatBox.jsx lines 208-235 render data-testid='chat-locked' with the EXACT text 'Complete Platform Fee Payment to Unlock Chat' + 'Payment Service Fee (5% + 18% GST)' explainer + payment_status pill if locked flag is true (computed from /api/chat/{bid}/access). UI layout, colour palette, spacing and sidebar unchanged from previous iteration — preserved.

issues: First moment after opening the modal the WS pill says '○ Reconnecting' for ~500-1500 ms before flipping to '● Live' — purely cosmetic / transient (backend handshake test confirms the WS opens immediately and emits 'presence'); consider showing the pill only after the first 1s to avoid flicker.

critical_code_review_comments: chat_routes._is_chat_unlocked() correctly treats 'unpaid' as locked and EVERY other payment_status (token_paid / completed / refunded) as unlocked — matches spec.

_check_access() centralises participant + payment gating; uses HTTPException(403, 'Chat Access Denied — Complete Platform Service Fee payment to unlock chat.') so all three REST endpoints (list/post/read) and the upload endpoint share the SAME error string. Excellent consistency for the FE to match against.
ws_chat (chat_routes.py line ~228) returns close code 4402 with reason 'payment_required' on locked bookings BEFORE accept() — so client never gets a half-open WS. Good.

iter9_routes.chat_upload (line ~351) re-implements the same gate independently with the SAME error string — but this is a code duplication smell. Consider importing _is_chat_unlocked from chat_routes (or moving the helper to a shared module) so future changes only touch one file.
iter11_routes.ai_search has a clean try/except around the LLM call AND a regex fallback that always returns a list. No 500 even if emergentintegrations is missing. Production-safe.

iter11_routes.my_bookings_csv & admin_revenue_csv both use the same artist_fee fallback formula `float(p.get('artist_fee', p.get('package_fee', 0) + p.get('addons_total', 0)))` — consistent with the iter10 admin_stats fix. Good.

iter11_routes.booking_ics builds a VEVENT with sensible defaults (event_time=19:00 fallback if missing, 3h duration). UID is booking-id + @booktalent → re-import safe / overwrites on calendar refresh.
ConnectionManager._ensure_redis is correctly lazy (no eager connect at import) — server boots fine without REDIS_URL. Confirmed by test_server_responsive.

Minor: CustomerDashboard.jsx line 230 — className uses `${chatUnlocked ? 'btn-ghost' : 'btn-ghost'}` ternary that resolves to the same class either way; either remove the ternary or change the unpaid case to a more disabled-looking class (e.g. btn-ghost-disabled). Functional but dead code.
Locked-card text in ChatBox.jsx line 225 reads 'Complete Platform Fee Payment to Unlock Chat' — matches spec exactly (case-sensitive match).
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_iter12.py (NEW — 18 tests: chat-gate REST + admin bypass + WebSocket close 4402 + paid WS presence + ai_search + ICS + customer CSV + admin revenue CSV + redis-less health)

success_rate.frontend: 100% on customer dashboard chat-gating (paid label/enabled/title + unpaid label/disabled/tooltip + paid modal opens + no chat-locked card on paid booking). Locked-card defence path confirmed by code review (data-testid='chat-locked' with exact spec text).

test_credentials: Admin admin@booktalent.com/Admin@123; Customer customer@booktalent.com/Customer@123 (16 bookings, paid=cca6a262-8393-4970-bd38-021dc13d52c7, unpaid=060a549a-0952-4425-84c0-422210ee501e); Artist priya@booktalent.com/Artist@123. All verified working.
seed_data_creation: No new seed data — used existing booking IDs from the spec.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 12 fully green. Re-run: `cd /app && python -m pytest backend/tests/test_iter12.py -v` (needs pip package `websocket-client` for the WS tests; already installed). Existing booking IDs PAID=cca6a262-8393-4970-bd38-021dc13d52c7, UNPAID=060a549a-0952-4425-84c0-422210ee501e. The 403 detail string is constant: 'Chat Access Denied — Complete Platform Service Fee payment to unlock chat.' — assert .startswith('Chat Access Denied') for forward-compat. WebSocket close code is 4402 (payment_required); some ws libs surface it only after recv(), so catch WebSocketBadStatusException OR check ws.close_code after recv. AI search endpoint is anonymous (no auth Depends) so it can be hit without token. ICS endpoint requires the booking to actually exist with event_date — paid booking has 2026-07-24 19:00 + venue 'Taj Hotel, Mumbai'. Customer CSV columns include 'Booking Ref' / 'Platform Service Fee' / 'GST'; admin CSV columns include 'Platform Fee (BT)'. Only one minor non-blocking cosmetic note: ChatBox WS pill shows 'Reconnecting' for a fraction of a second on modal open before going Live — not a regression.
rca of the issue: No outstanding bugs. Payment-gated chat (Iter 12) is implemented correctly across THREE surfaces: (1) Backend REST — chat_routes._check_access centralises the gate, returns 403 with the spec string; (2) Backend WebSocket — handshake closed with code 4402 before accept; (3) Frontend — CustomerDashboard renders the disabled '🔒 Pay to Unlock Chat' button with the correct tooltip for unpaid bookings, and ChatBox.jsx has a defence-in-depth locked card (data-testid='chat-locked') that would render if the modal were forced open on an unpaid booking. Admin bypass works on all gated endpoints (REST + WS). Iter 11 P3 endpoints (AI search, ICS, customer CSV, admin revenue CSV) all return 200 with the correct content-type / body shape / RBAC. Redis pubsub is lazy-init and the server boots cleanly without REDIS_URL.
